title: [FEAT] Implement Redis caching layer for GitHub API requests
body:
## Problem Statement
Currently, the application hits the GitHub API directly for every profile view. This leads to rate limiting (403 errors) when traffic spikes, leaving users with a broken page.

## Proposed Solution
Implement an Upstash Redis caching layer to store GitHub API responses. Use the `username` and endpoint as the cache key, with a TTL of 1 hour. If the cache misses, fetch from GitHub and populate the cache.

## Alternatives Considered
Next.js Data Cache (unstable in some Cloudflare environments), or Supabase Edge Functions. Upstash is preferred for its edge compatibility.

## Additional Context
Requires adding `@upstash/redis` and setting `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL` and `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN` in `.env.example`.

title: [REFACTOR] Optimize `fetchGitHubUser` with parallel requests
body:
## Problem Statement
Profile pages are loading slowly because we fetch user data, repositories, organizations, and contributions sequentially. 

## Proposed Solution
Refactor the data fetching logic in `src/app/[username]/page.tsx` and `src/lib/profile-data.ts` to use `Promise.all` or `Promise.allSettled`. This will allow all external GitHub API requests to fire simultaneously, significantly reducing the Time to First Byte (TTFB).

## Alternatives Considered
None. Parallelizing independent network requests is a standard optimization.

## Additional Context
Be mindful of error handling. If one promise fails (e.g., organizations API returns 500), it shouldn't crash the entire profile page.

title: [FEAT] Add 'Compare Profiles' feature
body:
## Problem Statement
Users want to compare their open-source stats (score, commits, PRs) side-by-side with their peers, but currently have to open two tabs to do so.

## Proposed Solution
Create a new route `/compare?user1=username&user2=username`. Build a side-by-side UI component that visually highlights who has a higher score, longer streak, and more PRs. 

## Alternatives Considered
An overlay modal on the profile page, but a dedicated URL allows users to share the comparison link easily.

## Additional Context
> If this is a UI feature, the contributor working on it should read `DESIGN.md` before starting.

title: [FEAT] Implement Webhooks to auto-update score on new push
body:
## Problem Statement
Scores and stats only update when a user manually triggers a refresh or views their profile after the cache expires. This makes the platform feel static.

## Proposed Solution
Set up a GitHub App / Webhook endpoint at `/api/webhooks/github`. When a user merges a PR or pushes code, GitHub pings our webhook, and we asynchronously recalculate their score and update Supabase in the background.

## Alternatives Considered
Polling the GitHub API via a cron job, but that is highly inefficient and uses too much API quota.

## Additional Context
Must verify the `X-Hub-Signature-256` header to ensure payloads actually come from GitHub.

title: [FEAT] Custom OG Image Generator for Twitter sharing
body:
## Problem Statement
When sharing an OSSfolio link on Twitter or LinkedIn, it shows a generic preview image. A personalized card would drive much higher engagement.

## Proposed Solution
Use `@vercel/og` to create a dynamic image endpoint at `/api/og?username=xxx`. The generated image should display the user's avatar, their total score, and their current streak using our brand colors.

## Alternatives Considered
Generating static PNGs on build, but that doesn't work for dynamic user data.

## Additional Context
Must use Edge Runtime for the OG route. Refer to `DESIGN.md` for fonts and colors.

title: [FEAT] Allow users to pin repositories on their profile
body:
## Problem Statement
The profile currently displays repositories sorted by stars automatically. Users might want to highlight a specific project that is less popular but more important to them.

## Proposed Solution
Add a 'Pin' toggle on repositories for authenticated users viewing their own profile. Save the pinned repository IDs in the `profiles` table in Supabase. On the profile page, render pinned repos at the top with a distinct border or icon.

## Alternatives Considered
Syncing directly with GitHub's pinned repos, but we want OSSfolio to have its own customization layer.

## Additional Context
Requires updating the Supabase schema to include a `pinned_repos` JSONB or text array column.

title: [FEAT] Display User Organizations
body:
## Problem Statement
Being part of notable open-source organizations (like Vercel, Facebook, or Apache) is a huge flex, but we don't display this on the profile.

## Proposed Solution
Fetch the user's organizations from the GitHub API (`/users/{username}/orgs`) and display their avatars in a horizontal flex container below the bio section. 

## Alternatives Considered
Listing organization names as text, but avatars are much more recognizable.

## Additional Context
> If this is a UI feature, the contributor working on it should read `DESIGN.md` before starting.

title: [FEAT] Global Leaderboard Page
body:
## Problem Statement
Users have scores, but no way to see how they rank against the rest of the OSSfolio community.

## Proposed Solution
Create a `/leaderboard` page that queries the top 100 users from the Supabase `profiles` table, ordered by `score` descending. Include pagination. 

## Alternatives Considered
Infinite scroll, but pagination is easier to implement and cache.

## Additional Context
Ensure the database query is optimized and perhaps cached so we don't hammer the database on every page load.

title: [FEAT] Add 'Top Languages' chart using Chart.js or Recharts
body:
## Problem Statement
It's hard to tell at a glance what a developer's primary tech stack is just by looking at their repo list.

## Proposed Solution
Aggregate language data across the user's repositories and display a minimalist pie chart or progress bar showing their top 3-5 languages. Use `recharts` for consistency.

## Alternatives Considered
Using GitHub's built-in language stats API, but it often includes vendor files. We should calculate it based on their owned repositories.

## Additional Context
Match the chart colors to standard GitHub language colors (e.g., Yellow for JS, Blue for TS).

title: [FEAT] Dark/Light Mode toggle
body:
## Problem Statement
The app is currently hardcoded to a dark theme. Some users prefer light mode for accessibility or readability.

## Proposed Solution
Integrate `next-themes` to support theme toggling. Update `globals.css` and `DESIGN.md` to define CSS variables for light mode variants of `canvas`, `ink`, and `ink-mute`.

## Alternatives Considered
Forcing system preference, but an explicit toggle in the navbar is better UX.

## Additional Context
Ensure no flash of unstyled content (FOUC) on load.

title: [FEAT] Custom Social Links
body:
## Problem Statement
Users can only display what's in their GitHub bio. They want to link out to their personal blog, Twitter, or LinkedIn.

## Proposed Solution
Add a section in `/settings` allowing users to add up to 4 custom URLs. Store these in a `custom_links` JSONB column in Supabase. Display them as small icons below the user's avatar on the profile page.

## Alternatives Considered
Parsing URLs directly from the bio text, but structured data is cleaner.

## Additional Context
Validate URLs before saving to prevent XSS or malicious links.

title: [FEAT] Achievements and Badges System
body:
## Problem Statement
To increase gamification, users need milestones to strive for beyond just a single score number.

## Proposed Solution
Implement a badge system (e.g., 'Night Owl' for late-night commits, 'Century' for 100+ PRs). Create a utility function that evaluates a user's GitHub stats and returns an array of unlocked badges. Render these in a dedicated grid on the profile.

## Alternatives Considered
Manual badge assignment, but automated calculation based on raw stats is scalable.

## Additional Context
Start with just 3 basic badges. We can add more later.

title: [FEAT] Activity Feed (Recent PRs)
body:
## Problem Statement
The contribution graph shows *when* a user was active, but not *what* they did.

## Proposed Solution
Use the GitHub Search API to find the user's most recently merged Pull Requests. Display a list of the 5 most recent PRs, showing the repo name, PR title, and relative time (e.g., '2 days ago').

## Alternatives Considered
Showing all events (issues, comments), but merged PRs represent the highest signal of OSS contribution.

## Additional Context
API Endpoint: `search/issues?q=author:USERNAME+type:pr+is:merged`

title: [FEAT] Customizable Headline
body:
## Problem Statement
Users often use their GitHub bio for generic info, but want a specific 'Headline' for OSSfolio to stand out to recruiters.

## Proposed Solution
Add a `headline` text column to the `profiles` table. Allow editing this via the `/settings` page. If set, this overrides the GitHub bio on their public profile.

## Alternatives Considered
None. Straightforward CRUD operation.

## Additional Context
Limit the headline to 160 characters. Provide a live preview in the settings UI.

title: [FEAT] User RSS Feed endpoint
body:
## Problem Statement
Developers want to syndicate their open-source activity to their personal blogs or newsletters.

## Proposed Solution
Create a dynamic route at `/[username]/rss.xml` that generates an RSS feed of the user's recent merged PRs and major open-source events. 

## Alternatives Considered
JSON Feed, but RSS is more universally supported by static site generators.

## Additional Context
Use the `rss` npm package to construct the XML. Ensure proper content-type headers.

title: [REFACTOR] Setup Turborepo for Monorepo architecture
body:
## Problem Statement
As we add more scripts, a Discord bot, or shared UI components, keeping everything in a single Next.js root directory will become messy.

## Proposed Solution
Migrate the repository to a Turborepo workspace. Move the Next.js app to `apps/web` and create shared packages in `packages/ui` and `packages/config`.

## Alternatives Considered
Nx, but Turborepo is generally easier to set up for Next.js-heavy codebases.

## Additional Context
This is a major structural change. Ensure all Vercel/Cloudflare build configurations are updated to point to `apps/web`.

title: [CHORE] Auto-generate Supabase types on build
body:
## Problem Statement
Currently, developers manually write TypeScript interfaces for database rows, leading to out-of-sync types when the schema changes.

## Proposed Solution
Integrate the Supabase CLI into the project. Add a `generate-types` script to `package.json` that fetches the schema from the local/remote project and outputs `database.types.ts`. Update the codebase to use these strict types.

## Alternatives Considered
Using Prisma, but we want to stick to the native Supabase client for edge compatibility.

## Additional Context
Requires setting up a local Supabase environment in Docker or defining a clear workflow in `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

title: [TEST] Implement Playwright E2E testing
body:
## Problem Statement
We have no automated testing to ensure the critical path (logging in, viewing a profile, updating settings) actually works before a deployment.

## Proposed Solution
Setup Playwright. Write tests to verify:
1. The homepage renders.
2. A mock user can view a generated profile.
3. A user can navigate to the leaderboard.
Integrate this into GitHub Actions on PRs.

## Alternatives Considered
Cypress, but Playwright is faster and has better support for modern web APIs.

## Additional Context
Do not test real GitHub OAuth in CI; mock the authentication state.

title: [CHORE] Setup Staging Environment on Pull Requests
body:
## Problem Statement
Reviewing UI changes is difficult when reviewers have to pull the code and run it locally.

## Proposed Solution
Configure Cloudflare Pages (or Vercel) to deploy a preview URL for every Pull Request. Comment the URL on the PR automatically using a GitHub Action.

## Alternatives Considered
None. Preview deployments are standard for modern web development.

## Additional Context
Ensure the staging environment connects to a separate staging Supabase instance so test data doesn't pollute production.

title: [CHORE] Add Husky and lint-staged for pre-commit hooks
body:
## Problem Statement
Contributors sometimes commit code with TypeScript errors or bad formatting, which fails CI and clutters the commit history.

## Proposed Solution
Install Husky and lint-staged. Configure a pre-commit hook that runs `eslint --fix` and `prettier --write` on staged files, and runs `tsc --noEmit` to catch type errors before the commit succeeds.

## Alternatives Considered
Only relying on CI, but failing fast locally provides better developer experience.

## Additional Context
Keep the hook fast. Only lint staged files, not the whole codebase.

title: [CHORE] Create a database seed script for local development
body:
## Problem Statement
New contributors have an empty database when they spin up the project locally, making it hard to test the leaderboard or profile views.

## Proposed Solution
Write a Node.js script (`scripts/seed.ts`) that uses the Supabase Admin client to inject 10 mock users and fake profile data into the local database.

## Alternatives Considered
Providing a SQL dump, but a TypeScript script allows generating dynamic dates and data via Faker.js.

## Additional Context
Add a command `npm run db:seed` to `package.json`.

title: [REFACTOR] Extract external API calls into a Services layer
body:
## Problem Statement
Data fetching logic (GitHub API, Supabase queries) is scattered directly inside React components and Page files, making it hard to test and reuse.

## Proposed Solution
Create a `src/services` directory. Move all `fetch` calls to GitHub into `services/github.ts` and Supabase queries into `services/db.ts`. Export clean asynchronous functions that the UI can consume.

## Alternatives Considered
Leaving it as is, but separation of concerns improves maintainability.

## Additional Context
Ensure types are strongly defined for the return values of these service functions.

title: [FEAT] Profile Visibility Toggles (Privacy)
body:
## Problem Statement
Currently, anyone who signs up has a public profile automatically. Some users might want to hide their profile from the leaderboard or make it completely private.

## Proposed Solution
Add a `visibility` enum column to `profiles` (`public`, `unlisted`, `private`). Update the settings UI with a radio group to select visibility. Ensure the leaderboard API filters out `unlisted` and `private`, and the profile page returns 404 for `private` unless the viewer is the owner.

## Alternatives Considered
Just a simple boolean `is_public`, but 3 states provide more control.

## Additional Context
Requires careful implementation of Row Level Security (RLS) in Supabase.

title: [FEAT] Rate limiting on the manual refresh endpoint
body:
## Problem Statement
A malicious user could spam the `/api/[username]/refresh` endpoint, burning through our GitHub API quota and causing denial of service.

## Proposed Solution
Implement rate limiting on the refresh endpoint using Upstash Redis or Cloudflare Workers KV. Limit users to 1 refresh per 5 minutes per IP or user ID. Return HTTP 429 Too Many Requests if exceeded.

## Alternatives Considered
In-memory rate limiting, but that resets on serverless cold starts and doesn't sync across edge nodes.

## Additional Context
Provide clear feedback in the UI when a user is rate-limited (e.g., disable the button and show a countdown).

title: [CHORE] Audit and strictify Supabase RLS policies
body:
## Problem Statement
Our Row Level Security (RLS) policies might be too permissive, potentially allowing users to modify another user's profile data via the client-side Supabase SDK.

## Proposed Solution
Review all SQL migrations. Ensure that `UPDATE` and `DELETE` operations on the `profiles` table strictly check `auth.uid() = id`. Write tests or a validation script to confirm policies are tight.

## Alternatives Considered
Moving all database mutations to server-side APIs, but leveraging RLS allows faster client-side mutations safely.

## Additional Context
Security is paramount. The PR must include detailed explanations of the policy changes.

title: [FEAT] Account Deletion
body:
## Problem Statement
GDPR and general privacy practices require that users have a clear way to permanently delete their account and associated data.

## Proposed Solution
Add a 'Danger Zone' section in `/settings` with a 'Delete Account' button. Requires typing their username to confirm. When triggered, call an API endpoint that deletes their Supabase auth record and wipes the `profiles` row.

## Alternatives Considered
Soft deletion (setting a flag), but hard deletion is better for compliance.

## Additional Context
Ensure the cascade deletes work correctly in the database so no orphaned data is left behind.

title: [FEAT] Implement loading skeletons for profile page
body:
## Problem Statement
While data is fetching, the page is blank or unstyled. We need a smoother perceived performance.

## Proposed Solution
Create React suspense boundaries and build skeleton loader components (using pulsing CSS animations) that match the shape of the avatar, stats boxes, and heatmap. 

## Alternatives Considered
A simple loading spinner, but skeletons provide a much more premium feel.

## Additional Context
> Read `DESIGN.md` for standard border radii and spacing to make the skeletons match the final UI perfectly.

title: [BUG] Heatmap renders incorrectly on mobile viewports
body:
## Problem Statement
The contribution heatmap overflows its container horizontally on screens smaller than 600px, breaking the page layout and causing horizontal scrolling.

## Proposed Solution
Implement a responsive design strategy for the heatmap. Either wrap the SVG in a scrollable container (`overflow-x: auto`), or conditionally render fewer months of data based on the window width using a ResizeObserver or CSS media queries.

## Alternatives Considered
Scaling down the SVG, but that makes the individual squares too small to tap or read.

## Additional Context
Test on a simulated iPhone SE viewport to ensure it fits perfectly.

title: [FEAT] Add OpenGraph metadata for dynamic routes
body:
## Problem Statement
When pasting a link to a specific repository comparison or the leaderboard, messaging apps display a generic fallback image instead of context-specific metadata.

## Proposed Solution
Implement Next.js `generateMetadata` exports in `src/app/leaderboard/page.tsx` and `src/app/compare/page.tsx`. Ensure the title and description dynamically reflect the content of the page.

## Alternatives Considered
Static metadata, but dynamic is required for good SEO and shareability.

## Additional Context
This involves updating the `<head>` tags implicitly through Next.js conventions.

title: [REFACTOR] Migrate CSS to TailwindCSS v4 features
body:
## Problem Statement
We recently upgraded to TailwindCSS v4, but we are still using older v3 paradigms and arbitrary values where native theme tokens or CSS variables could be used more efficiently.

## Proposed Solution
Refactor `globals.css` and component classNames to utilize Tailwind v4's new CSS-first configuration and dynamic utility features. Remove any deprecated plugins or workarounds.

## Alternatives Considered
Staying with the current syntax, but migrating keeps the codebase modern and easier to maintain.

## Additional Context
Ensure no visual regressions occur. Compare the UI before and after the refactor.
